summary: Iteration 3 testing: Found & fixed a CRITICAL backend bug in log_audit() (`return doc` referenced undefined `doc` → NameError → 500 on every admin PUT/POST/DELETE). After the fix, 39/39 backend pytest tests pass (added TestPromo, TestSocialProof, TestWATemplates, TestSubscribers, TestAudit, TestUpload — 17 new tests; existing 22 still pass). Frontend: PromoBanner with 4-cell countdown + close button renders, SocialProofToast rotates messages with close button, EmailSignup section works end-to-end (returns DUKKANK10-XXXXXX code with copy button), Admin has all 7 tabs with all 18 marketing testids OK, Audit tab shows 29 recent entries with search+refresh, Games edit dialog has game-image-upload-button + hidden file input + URL input.

critical.issue: log_audit() had `return doc` after try/except where `doc` is undefined → NameError → 500 on every admin write. FIXED by removing the stray `return doc`. Audit entries were still being inserted (insert_one runs before the NameError), but the calling endpoint then crashed with 500 to the client.

integration_issues.flow: EmailSignup section never renders by default
integration_issues.issue: App.js renders EmailSignup only via SECTION_RENDERERS['emailSignup'] inside the visibleSections.map loop, but server.py DEFAULT_SECTIONS does NOT include an 'emailSignup' entry. So fresh installs / after section reset, the section is invisible. To verify in UI testing I had to PUT /api/admin/sections with an 'emailSignup' item prepended. Recommend adding {id:'emailSignup', label:'اشترك بالنشرة', visible:true} to DEFAULT_SECTIONS OR rendering <EmailSignup /> outside the sections loop (like PromoBanner and SocialProofToast).

issues: Field labels are partially obscured (overlapping with the dark background) — the contrast / positioning of the labels above each input is hard to read in the dialog.

action_items: [VERIFY FIX] log_audit `return doc` was removed by testing agent; confirm fix lands and there are no other accidental references. (root cause: stale code from earlier edit)

Add 'emailSignup' entry to DEFAULT_SECTIONS in /app/backend/server.py so the new section appears for fresh users, OR render <EmailSignup /> unconditionally in App.js (outside the SECTION_RENDERERS map).
Add data-testid to the per-game edit button in GamesTab (e.g., `game-edit-{id}`) — currently must rely on pencil-svg fallback which is fragile.
Optional UX polish: dark dialog labels in GamesTab edit dialog are hard to read.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py `log_audit()` had `return doc` referring to an undefined variable (likely leftover from copy of strip_id). This single character-of-leftover-code broke EVERY admin write endpoint. Suggest adding a smoke test that does an admin PUT on /api/admin/promo as part of CI.

Default sections list and emailSignup decoupling — App.js gates rendering on the persisted /api/sections doc, which means any new section must also be added to DEFAULT_SECTIONS or it never appears for users who already have a persisted sections doc. The reset-to-default workflow then erases the new section if users reset.

All input validation looks fine (Pydantic models), image upload extension whitelist + 5MB cap are sensible, audit log is fire-and-forget. CORS uses split env var with `*` default — acceptable for now.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (added TestPromo, TestSocialProof, TestWATemplates, TestSubscribers, TestAudit, TestUpload — 17 new tests)

success_rate.frontend: 100% on visible flows after sections workaround

seed_data_creation: Test subscribers (TEST_iter3_*@example.com) are created and cleaned up in TestSubscribers. After UI test, the test email was DELETEd. State has been reset: promo disabled, sections back to 9 defaults, social_proof reset to 10 default messages, wa_templates restored to originals.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py now has 39 tests (was 22). The log_audit `return doc` bug must remain fixed — if it regresses, ALL admin endpoints return 500 even though writes go through. The emailSignup section is NOT in DEFAULT_SECTIONS — main agent should fix this; until then UI tests need to PUT sections with an emailSignup entry to make the section render. Subscribers collection may contain old test@example.com — safe to ignore. Audit log accumulates entries across runs.
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause: a leftover `return doc` line inside async def log_audit() referenced an undefined name `doc` (likely stale code from copy-pasting strip_id). Python only raises the NameError when execution reaches that line, which happens on every successful audit insert. Because `log_audit` is awaited from inside each admin endpoint, the exception propagates up and FastAPI returns 500 to the client — even though the DB insert succeeded. Reproduction: PUT /api/admin/promo with valid bearer → 500 + 'Internal Server Error' (pre-fix); audit entry visible in /api/admin/audit. Mitigation: removed the stray `return doc`. Added regression test (TestAudit.test_audit_records_admin_write + TestPromo.test_put_promo_persists_and_reset) that exercises an admin PUT which would have surfaced this on next iteration.
